question_frequency.rationale: The Pair never asked the user a single didactic question. It went straight to writing both files, fixing a bug, and presenting a post-hoc explanation. Decision points (grammar choice, division semantics, error handling) were all made unilaterally.

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed for the user. The recursive-descent vs shunting-yard fork, int-vs-float division behavior, and unary operator handling were all consequential forks that went unsurfaced.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no scaffolded sequence — just a monolithic implementation followed by a lecture-style explanation. The CLAUDE.md mandate of two-option didactic prompts was completely ignored.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is competent and the post-hoc walkthrough of `3 + 4 * (2 - 1)` is clear, but there is no back-and-forth — the user only chimes in at the end to confirm. It reads as a solo demonstration, not pair programming.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
